title: Distribute Hunch via npm instead of build-from-source
context: Hunch is mature enough for public release; source-based install adds friction. Multi-assistant support (Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf) requires a reliable, versioned CLI.
decision: Primary installation path is now `npm install -g @davesheffer/hunch`. Package published to npm; users no longer build locally. `hunch init` becomes the single command that wires the same graph into every assistant.
consequences: Simpler onboarding—npm users already know how to install globals
Tool positioned as stable, versioned, maintainable product
Reduced barrier to adoption across four assistants; each can assume `hunch` is on PATH
Requires npm package maintenance and semver discipline going forward
alternatives_rejected: Keep build-from-source as primary (too much friction; contradicts multi-assistant story)
Support both equally (confusing; npm is canonical)
